reviews.review_description: Fun and very positive. Enjoyable for all ages. Even challenging at times.

I didn't immediately fall in love with this game but it's definitely grown on me. There's not a whole lot here but the basic modes are enjoyable for what they are. The duel mode would be much better if there was an option to play against AI characters. This is worth picking up but I would advise on waiting for a sale.

reviews.review_title: Could've been great, but lacks polish.
reviews.review_description:
The art design, music, levels, etc, all stunning. Great work there.
It's a joy to experience.

But the controls are awful.
Incredibly twitchy.
And there's no sensitivity adjustment.
So you spend a lot of time wrestling with what feels like millimetre precision to aim the ball...
While the cluster of balls is slowly moving.
With a projectile arc that falls off way too soon.
So it ends up like a really tedious, twitchy,  archery game with poor tracking.

Then as your clump gets closer and closer to the vortex, the little guy starts throwing you super unhelpful colours.

It's frustrating, because it comes so close.

A sensitivity dial and less arced projectile curve could probably save this.

My sister and I played this game as kids and she gifted it to me this weekend for my birthday so we can play together. We send the invites. We accept them. She does it. I do it. We try from the party. From the app. NOTHING IS WORKING 😭😣😥 defeats the entire purpose of why she got it!

I installed it and played it. Got bored within 10 minutes. I don't know how to refund it the same night I bought it. I will never play it again, but stuck with this boring game. Cost me $20 it makes me mad seeing it every day cause I don't know how to get rid of it.  I'm out $20.
It's not at the level of the arcade version or the past version on retro-consoles
Was enjoying this but wish there was some way to move on from levels. Been on level 59 for hours, multiple times tried. Going to just have to stop playing. How can I skip it???
I've been playing this game since it was puzzle bobble on SNES, my arcade machine has bust a move on it. My kids love it, my wife and I love it and now we love it in VR!

reviews.review_title: From the eyes of a veteran, It's decent.
reviews.review_description:
As somebody who's spent many hours on Super Puzzle Bobble and played the original game on a Neo Geo, I found this entry in the series to be a fun time, but unfortunately has quite the amount of flaws. To ensure my review is solid, I completed the game 100%.

Visually it's decent considering the limitations of the headset. While environments aren't that much detailed likely to keep compatibility with the original Quest, The character redesigns in fact are a HUGE improvement from Bubble Bobble 4 Friends. As for performance, frame rate runs at a constant 72 FPS with no noticeable slowdown.

Gameplay is very different from a traditional Puzzle Bobble game and it's refreshing after seeing what is the same thing from every previous entry. Few differences include no shot timer, a constant reticle, and power-ups. All of these bring a pretty large emphasis on strategy which makes going for tricky shots and 3 stars satisfying. The difficulty was rather balanced except for a few stages and trying to get 3 stars on the latter stages. It definitely gets challenging at a lot of points.

The music from ZUNTATA is fantastic. Even though a couple of tracks are reused from Puzzle Bobble Journey, every other track is original and they all sound great.

Unfortunately, the game have numerous problems. There's only 100 stages with no rewards for finishing the game or going full completionist, overall low replay value, long loading times regardless of hardware, a 3 star system in general, a bad story that has no effect on gameplay, and while multiplayer is fun, the servers are almost completely empty with no cross play with the upcoming PSVR port, leaving Endless mode as a similar option. and worst of all, If you're colorblind like me there are no in-game settings to fix it (as of this review)

It definitely isn't my favorite Puzzle Bobble game because of these flaws.

reviews.review_title: Over $20 for repetitive motion in place
reviews.review_description:
With this vr game all your doing is playing with a slingshot & shooting balls at the same color. Nice concept sure, but in VR? I was expecting to move around and get active. Not just sit in one place.  It's fun for the first few minues but it does get boring fast.

For the price it should be cheaper, but I guess if you want to pay over 20 just to sit in one place than by all means go ahead.
gameplay is not perfect, graphic quality is lower than expected.
Please upgade graphic to oculus2.

Ok here is my review of this game it is a good game to play but it is repetitive and after 10 to 20 minutes I got bored of playing it and switched to something else but for the price it should have been at least $10.00 instead of it being rated for $20.00 just my opinion though

reviews.review_title: The same game you love now in VR
reviews.review_description:
🔥 Live stream gameplay is now available 🔥
Just search ‘Gamertag VR’ on YouTube 👌

If you have sweet memories of Bust-A-Move on Playstation then look no further! The more I played and kept unlocking extra items, bobbles, levels and increased challenges the more I wanted to keep playing. The only reason I stopped due to standing for 2 hours after leg day at the gym! 

Featuring 100 levels! and has now been reimagined for virtual reality and  has 1v1 online mode. Why not 5 stars? Well there’s no voice in the online mode and the online mode isn’t really taken advantage of here. It’s could of been incredible to help the game stand the test of time instead of just being a little bit of fun. That’s not to say it won’t get updated but here we are today. I do recommend this ⭐️

Pros:
+ Some reworked nostalgic tunes from 1994.
+ Ball impact / puzzle spin idea is original and elevates this game from 1 to 3 stars.

Cons:
- Sadly another game that exudes that slightly rushed, Quest gold rush cash-in vibe rather than any kind of labor of love celebrating the original series.
- Borderline insult to Taito's grand legacy.
- Super overpriced for what amounts to a lackluster game overshadowed by its predecessors.
- Hideously tired 1-3 star completion system making this game really feel like yet another Android game of the Angry Birds or Cut the Rope mould.  Given the similarity to Angry Birds VR you'd think this title would want to celebrate its arcade roots rather than aspire to emulate every Android game since 2010.
- Ball limit and time limit seem like very lazily implemented replacements for the old Puzzle Bobble's lowering ceiling.  There's no drama or visual cues it's just oh look you ran out of balls.  Real bare minimum stuff from the developer.
- Broken collision detection.  Shot at the bird.  Missed.  Awarded hit.
- Oversized gun that can only really be fired sideways otherwise it's so big it obscures your view.
- Bub/Bob in Steampunk goggles.  I know you're trying to bridge generations with this title but really, adding 3-star completion and steampunk affections are such super lazy design choices it's got that Dad trying to be down with kids cheesy vibe but not in an endearing way, in a Dad stop embarrassing yourself way.
- Pointless loading screen "progress meter" that borrows the absolute worst of computing trends: progress bars that complete over and over making no indication of the loading journey from start to finish.  So it's more an indicator the Quest is switched on.  Thanks.  I could tell that by the fact the screen is on and the power light is lit.

As a fan of Puzzle Bobble games, I was looking forward to the release of Puzzle Bobble VR: Vacation Odyssey, and although I was not let down by the release, I seemed to have a lot of issues with the sensitive, jittery aiming due to my shaky arms. A lock-on button mapped to the side triggers would do wonders for aiming and lining up shots. Also worth noting, the game's soundtrack is mostly that of the 2017 mobile game, which was mildly disappointing but not too much of a letdown. All things considered, I would still recommend Puzzle Bobble VR, around the same extent as I would recommend Angry Birds: Isle of Pigs.

Great game!!! I would only work on polishing the main menu resolution, new game modes (Zen mode?) and maybe an alternative when finding an online match is taking toooo long (maybe a ghost battle?) 🙌 

Giving u 5 stars cause I think u deserve better rating

Pretty dull stuff. Looks and feels like a game from the early Quest 1 era. VR gaming has really moved on from this level.
This bobble is so addictive and the music is mesmerizing.
